text:
Joey Woelber In the original Japanese difficulty of Mega Man 2 (labeled "difficult" mode overseas), Metal Man dies in two hits to the Metal Blade.

In the Normal Mode of the toned-down version given to us American babies (which is more like an easy mode since "Difficult" is the original difficulty), it kills him instantly.

text:
Joey Woelber Oh.

In issue 11, Mega Man fights Metal Man, copies the Metal Blade in the middle of the fight, and kills him with it. He also finishes off the Wily Machine with it in issue 12 with a quip about how easy it would’ve been if he used Metal Blade from the start.

Furthermore, in the Ra Moon arc, when Metal Man, Flash Man, Bubble Man and Heat Man ambush Mega Man and a few of the MM1 Robot Masters, Rock yet again copies Metal Man's weapon data mid-fight before immediately wasting him with Metal Blade.

text: Mr. Loowiz Trust me, sir.  There are people out there on the internet who enjoy giving very verbose comments on just menial things that exist out there.  I myself have no problems giving a 1-line troll a paragraph repose to read through.  There are also people like me who will find it entirely entertaining to post a multi-paragraph response to something.  I have absolutely no bearing on this and no care about anyone related, but the amount of ignorance a person who uses proper caps and punctuation can show somewhat irritated me just a little.  Given now days I am able to post as much as I want without restraint or refrain, why should I not post several lines and/or paragraphs to something silly, hmm?  Nothing here really bothers me, but trust me, I'll pad out the length of this to suit myself. ;D

Unfortunately for him, Pharaohman had lifted more than just his appearance from the Egyptian kings of old; he was just as ruthless as they could be, and, taking Megaman's comparatively weaker armor into account,  rewarded Megaman's prowess with a punch in the face, courtesy of his generously proportioned fists.

text:
@AspecZzcWr 

In the Japanese games his name was Rockman, so he was named off the "Rock" part. But in the English versions his name was still Rock but they called him Mega Man instead. Where it gets confusing is that in the English version of Powered Up, Mega Man was so named because his name was Mega instead of Rock. (Capcom later changed this back to his name being Rock but him still being called Mega Man though).
